name: cpu_dataframe
title: Analytics query engine
short_description: Group, join and sort over a bundled columnar dataset.

structured_text.description: Runs a set of analytics queries over a bundled columnar dataset using an in process query engine, covering grouping, joining and sorting across all cores. The dataset and queries are fixed, and the engine keeps everything in memory so the result reflects the processor and its memory system.
structured_text.metric_explanation: The score is the number of rows processed per second across the query set, reported as operations per second.
structured_text.value_interpretation: Higher is better. More rows per second means interactive analysis over larger tables without long pauses between queries.
structured_text.reasoning: Grouping, joining and sorting are the heart of tabular analytics, and they stress both core throughput and memory bandwidth in the way real data tools do.

domain_gaming.explanation: Games do little tabular analytics during play.

domain_office.explanation: Spreadsheets and reports run smaller versions of the same grouping work.

domain_software_development.explanation: Developers query logs and metrics with the same engine style.

domain_graphics_design.explanation: Design work involves little data analytics.

domain_video_editing.explanation: Editing does not depend on query workloads.

domain_3d_rendering.explanation: Rendering pipelines rarely run analytics queries.

domain_audio_production.explanation: Audio work has little tabular analysis.

domain_cad_engineering.explanation: Engineering teams analyse simulation result tables.

domain_scientific_computing.explanation: Result analysis often runs through the same grouping and joining kernels.

domain_ai_training.explanation: Feature engineering over large tables is a core part of preparing training data.

domain_ai_inference.explanation: Batch scoring pipelines join and aggregate inputs around the model.

domain_data_science.explanation: This is the central daily workload for data analytics.

domain_server_virt.explanation: Analytics services run these queries continuously on shared servers.
